兵工廠4強遇舊帥 阿迪達向艾馬利送高帽

【Now Sports】阿仙奴晉身歐霸盃4強後將會鬥由主領隊艾馬利領軍的維拉利爾，阿迪達不忘向前輩派高帽，一於先禮後「兵」。
阿仙奴於周四歐霸盃以4:0大炒布拉格斯拉維亞（兩回合計勝5:1），領隊阿迪達先讚賞球員表現出色：「我們開局表現極具說服力，高壓而進取，全部時間都具威脅。我實在喜歡球隊在入球被判作廢後所作出的反應，之後取得出色的入球。堅壁清野亦重要，這是連續兩仗所做到。」

阿仙奴將於4強惡鬥由前領隊艾馬利領軍的維拉利爾，後者於2019年曾帶領阿仙奴殺入歐霸盃決賽（負車路士1:4），而他執教西維爾時曾3度贏得歐霸盃。阿迪達對他推崇不已：「這將會很艱難，艾馬利也許是這個賽事最成功的主帥。」

「面前還有很多事情要去做並加以改善，我們有決心改善，盼一天比一天好。」阿迪達續說。

圖片來源 : ©AFP

阿仙奴將於4強惡鬥由前領隊艾馬利領軍的維拉利爾，後者於2019年曾帶領阿仙奴殺入歐霸盃決賽（負車路士1:4），而他執教西維爾時曾3度贏得歐霸盃。阿迪達對他推崇不已：「這將會很艱難，艾馬利也許是這個賽事最成功的主帥。」

「面前還有很多事情要去做並加以改善，我們有決心改善，盼一天比一天好。」阿迪達續說。

Xhaka – I don't care which position I play

For the second time in five days Granit Xhaka lined up at left back, and just like at Sheffield United, he played his part in a victory and a clean sheet.

The stakes were much higher on Thursday though, against a Slavia Prague side that hadn’t lost at home for two years – a run stretching over 30 games in all competitions.

And as we took another step towards a possible European final, Xhaka said he is willing to carry on filling in at full-back if needs be.

“If we win the Europa League like this, I don't care which position I play!” he told Arsenal.com after the final whistle.

“If I have to help the team there, I have to help. I'm the guy who will do everything for the team. Of course, everyone knows it's not my regular position but with the team-mates, they help me and coach me as well, in front and behind. I'm so happy I helped the team in this position as well. I try my best and in the last two games it was a good performance.

'It's a joy to watch them play'

Bukayo Saka and Emile Smith Rowe were at their free-flowing best on Thursday night.

Lining up behind Alex Lacazette in an exciting forward line, our Hale End graduates broke free from the Slavia Prague defence time and time again to help us book our place in the Europa League semi-finals.

And Mikel Arteta was full of praise for our teenagers after the game.

"For me, it's a joy to watch them play and to watch them every day in training," he said. "How they behave, the passion and the commitment they have for the club and how much they love to play football.

"You have to let them express themselves and you have to give them certain room to work, and some ideas of how they can exploit their qualities. Then it's up to them. I thought both of them were really good today.

"Players have to make each other better. That's a big quality. Then you need somebody at the back to make those players better and get the surroundings, the collective understanding, the way it should be.

"The same happened with the backline. Today we looked really solid and I don't think we conceded a shot on target. That's maybe because of a little bit of continuity or a little bit of help in these understanding moments."

Many of us were new and we managed to know each other better, then the Champions League elimination allowed us to focus only on Serie A".

Scudetto could be the nearest future for Inter and the Moroccan player: "We are talking about it (scudetto, ndr) but with respect, we know we haven't won yet. We are not hiding because it's the clear target, but there are still a lot of matches left". Inter count a lot on the amazing class 1998 fullback, who's been involved as testimonial in the campaign for the launch of new Inter logo: "It's been an honour, I like it because it looks to the future, a future that we could build together. I'm happy here, I hope to stay with this club for a long time".

Real Madrid make Martin Odegaard decision

Real Madrid are not open to selling Martin Odegaard this summer in a story that seems to swing back and forth every week.

After six years at the club, Martin Odegaard’s future is far from certain.

Depending on when or where you have done your reading this season, you likely think Real Madrid will sell Odegaard to Arsenal, they’ll let him go out on loan again, they won’t sell him at all or they haven’t made a decision yet.

Arsenal want him and it’s not hard to see why. His quality is evident and, at only 22, he has his whole career ahead of him.

According to Marca, Odegaard is now seen as one of Real Madrid’s ‘untouchables’ and will not be leaving the club.

Although Real Madrid need to raise cash to buy Erling Haaland from Borussia Dortmund this summer, they are not planning to sell Odegaard to help with that move.

It did always seem like a difficult deal for Arsenal to pull off.

If Odegaard impressed enough that Mikel Arteta wanted to sign him permanently, that was always going to make things either more expensive or impossible. If he performed at a level that made Real Madrid want to sell him, Arsenal wouldn’t really want him either.

As things stand, it seems unlikely Odegaard will be an Arsenal player beyond the end of this season, but, as we noted when he first moved here, Odegaard took a shirt number he knew he could keep, so perhaps the player will have the final say this summer.

Pierre-Emerick Aubameyang
